name: GovOps Design System
concept: Statute meets System — law-as-code made legible. Two registers (legal + machine) coexist with provenance always visible (lavender = agent, gold = human authority).
audience: Multilingual, agentic civic software. RTL-first capable. WCAG 2.2 AA minimum.

color._note: All colors in OKLCH for perceptual uniformity. Hex provided as fallback for non-CSS contexts.

parchment._note: Warm off-white series. Use as base in light mode and as inverse surface in dark mode.

lavender._role: AGENTIC. Marks anything proposed, drafted, suggested, or executed by an agent.

civic-gold._role: HUMAN AUTHORITY. Marks human enactment, ratification, signature, override.

verdict._note: Status colors. Muted, civic — not SaaS-bright.

semantic._note: Token name → primitive reference. The app should consume ONLY semantic tokens, never primitives directly.

provenance._note: ALWAYS visible. Every artifact in the system carries a provenance ribbon.

typography._strategy: Three-register stack: Serif (legal), Sans (UI), Mono (rules/code). Each register has multilingual + RTL coverage.

serif.use: Statute text, preamble, long-form policy reading, hero headlines

sans.use: All UI: buttons, labels, navigation, tables, forms
sans.rationale: Inter has wide Latin/Cyrillic/Greek coverage; fall back to Noto Sans subsets for everything else. Load per-locale via @font-face unicode-range.

mono.use: Rule code, agent traces, IDs, citations (§ §123(4)(a)), diff views, tokens

rules: Serif headlines + sans body (never the inverse).
Statute body text uses the 'statute' size — generous leading, serif, max 65ch line length.
Rule code and citations always mono, never italicized.
RTL: keep numerals in Latin OR use locale-appropriate (Arabic-Indic) — decide per locale, document choice.
Never combine more than 2 weights of any one family on a single screen.

space._rtl_note: Always use logical properties: padding-inline-start NOT padding-left; margin-inline-end NOT margin-right; inset-inline; border-inline-start. Tailwind: ps-*, pe-*, ms-*, me-*, start-*, end-*.

radius._note: Use sm/md for civic feel. Avoid 2xl+ except for avatars and pills — over-rounded reads as consumer SaaS, not institution.

shadow._strategy: Subtle. Civic, not floaty. Most surfaces use border, not shadow.

motion._principle: Deliberate, never decorative. Motion signals causation: agent acts → ribbon pulses; human ratifies → gold seal stamps.

agent-proposing.effect: lavender ribbon fades in + types out content

human-enacting.effect: gold seal scales 0.9→1, opacity 0→1, single thump

diff-reveal.effect: old text strikes through, new text underlines in

motion.reduced-motion: Honor prefers-reduced-motion: replace all transforms/scales with opacity-only crossfades at duration.fast.

provenance-ribbon._description: Persistent vertical bar on the inline-start edge of any artifact card. 3px wide. Color from provenance token. Tooltip on hover shows author + timestamp.

diff-view._description: Default rendering for ANY policy/rule change. Side-by-side on >=768px, stacked below.

citation._description: Inline reference to a statute, e.g. § 12(3)(a). Mono, civic-gold underline on hover, opens drawer with full text.

agent-trace._description: Collapsible reasoning log shown under any agent action. Mono. Lavender left rule. Token-by-token reveal during streaming.

i18n.html_dir: Set <html dir='rtl|ltr' lang='...'> at root from negotiated locale.
rules: Use CSS logical properties everywhere — never left/right.
Mirror navigation, breadcrumbs, progress bars, sliders. Never mirror media, code blocks, charts axes, clock icons.
Plurals via ICU MessageFormat (not just one/other).
Numbers, dates, currency via Intl.* APIs with locale.
Statute citations remain in source script (an Arabic statute keeps Arabic-Indic § numbering).
Allow 30% text expansion budget on all UI containers.
Provide a language switcher in the masthead, persisted per user.
i18n.fonts_loading: Use unicode-range subsetting + font-display:swap. Lazy-load CJK/Arabic subsets only when locale requires.

accessibility.target: WCAG 2.2 AA, working toward AAA on statute reading surfaces

contrast.body_text_on_background: ≥ 7:1 (AAA) — verified for both light and dark themes

focus.visible: Always. 3px ring using ring-focus shadow, 2px offset from element.
focus.never: Do not remove outlines without replacement. Do not rely on color alone.

accessibility.color_independence: Provenance is ALWAYS color + icon + label. Never color alone (colorblind, monochrome printing of statute).
accessibility.keyboard: Every action reachable via keyboard. Skip-to-content link on every page. Modal focus trap mandatory.

aria.agent_action_buttons: aria-label must include 'proposed by agent' equivalent

accessibility.language: Every page has correct lang attr. Inline language switches use lang on the element.

principles: Plain language first; legal precision second; jargon never.
Name the actor. 'The agent drafted…' / 'You ratified…' / 'The council enacted…'. Never passive 'was created'.
Show provenance in copy, not just color. 'Drafted by Agent · Awaiting your review.'
Past tense for enacted law. Present tense for active policy. Future conditional for proposals.

concept_notes: Wordmark: serif 'Gov' meets mono/geometric 'Ops' on a single baseline. Variant: render the 'O' as a zero ('Gov0ps') to embed the law-as-code idea directly into the mark.
Symbol: § fused with { } as a single monoline glyph — statute and system as one form.
Clear-space: ½ × cap-height on all sides. Never place on busy photography without a solid plate.
Minimum sizes: wordmark 96px wide; symbol 24px square.
Color: ink on parchment (default), parchment on ink (dark), single-color print only (no gradients on the mark).
